hero.title: Turning Data into Strategy, and Ideas into Impact.
hero.intro:
I've never done things on the typical timeline, and that's my greatest asset. I skipped a grade, graduated at 16, and spent my gap year traveling and volunteering before I ever stepped into a classroom. I have always moved with ambition, but never without intention.

Every summer since, I've shown up somewhere new. Dallas with AT&T. New York City with L'Oréal. Portugal on an academic program. Each one added a new lens and made me sharper in rooms I had never been in before.

At VCU, I study Marketing, Data Analytics, and Sales, but my real education has been in the work: building partnerships with Fortune 500 companies, launching a fellowship from the ground up, and leading a 78-member organization as Executive President.

I'm obsessed with brands that make people feel something and partnerships that create real mutual value. The strategy behind the story, who we're talking to, why it matters, how to make it land. That's what drives me.

jobs.blurb: At Extern, I drive the full partnership sales cycle from prospecting to close, independently securing strategic collaborations with major global brands. I cultivate a high volume of client relationships through LinkedIn and email outreach, focused on accelerating account growth and hitting quarterly goals. I also pioneer AI agent workflows to sharpen outbound efficiency, building smarter systems for research, lead qualification, and outreach.

jobs.company: VCU Office of Innovation & Strategic Design
jobs.meta: Innovation & Strategic Design Fellow | Richmond, VA
jobs.blurb: As the inaugural Innovation and Strategic Design Fellow at VCU's Da Vinci Center, I am architecting a brand new fellowship program from the ground up. I spearhead business development through digital marketing campaigns and targeted outreach to forge partnerships with Fortune 500 companies. I also orchestrate Design Jams and lead recruitment efforts, connecting students with university leadership and state government stakeholders.

jobs.meta: Omnichannel Education & Sales Intern | New York City, NY
jobs.blurb: At L'Oréal, I spearheaded comprehensive competitive intelligence research across the beauty industry, dissecting product offerings, pricing strategies, and market positioning to shape executive-level strategy decisions. I stewarded vendor relationships and drove account operations through Salesforce CRM, and crafted a DEI business case study that championed strategic expansion into underserved market segments.

jobs.meta: Senior Associate Marketing Intern | Dallas, TX
jobs.blurb: Senior Associate Marketing Intern At AT&T, I engineered data-driven campaign recommendations using KPI analysis and financial projections to sharpen budget allocation and maximize ROI. I also conceived and produced the monthly newsletter for AT&T's Mid Markets division, covering company updates, events, and corporate social responsibility initiatives to unite internal teams and drive awareness.

items.name: Alpha Kappa Psi Fraternity
items.blurb: As Executive President I lead and mentor a 78-member professional business fraternity chapter, managing an executive board of 7 direct reports across operations, recruitment, alumni relations, and strategic planning. Through data-informed outreach and recruitment strategy I drove a 40% increase in new member growth year over year while overseeing a $20,000 annual budget allocated across professional development and chapter programming.

items.name: Student Managed Investment Portfolio (SMIP)
items.blurb: SMIP began a decade ago when VCU entrusted our student-run club with a $100,000 endowment to manage independently, and through long-term investment strategy that portfolio has grown to $415,000. As an Investment Analyst I conduct equity research on Consumer Staples companies using Bloomberg Terminal and IBISWorld to inform buy and sell decisions, collaborating weekly with 40 analysts to evaluate market conditions and present data-driven investment pitches that shape the portfolio's direction.

gapYear.title: Gap Year Journey
gapYear.blurb: Before college, I spent a year living in Hawaii and Colombia, volunteering with local organizations and immersing myself in communities that had nothing to do with my career path and everything to do with who I was becoming. In Hawaii, I worked alongside conservation groups on beach and trail cleanups, learning what it looks like to show up consistently for something with no immediate payoff. In Colombia, I taught English through community education programs, where I saw firsthand how access to language can quietly change the trajectory of someone's life. That year was not on any plan I had made. But it taught me how to build trust with people from entirely different walks of life, how to find purpose in unglamorous work, and how to move through the world with a little more intention than I had before.
